body: This would remove the earlier check for UDF support for GSQL dialect. Is that check not needed for GSQL? UDF is supported for all DBs in GSQL dialect?
Wow. Please add a comment mentioning the difference between PG dialect and GSQL with an example.
Here as well. Please add a comment illustrating the difference with an example.
Do we not need the parentheses for PG dialect?

I am assuming you have covered all the possible cases here. I'm relying on your domain expertise to cover all branches. For example: UDFs with no params, 1 params, multiple, with all the different determinisms etc.

If not covered. Please cover them fully.

diff_hunk:
@@ -1035,9 +1035,19 @@ private void listUdfs(Ddl.Builder builder) {
                     + " AND r.routine_type = 'FUNCTION'"
                     + " AND r.routine_body = 'SQL'");
         break;
+      case POSTGRESQL:
+        queryStatement =
+            Statement.of(
+                "SELECT r.routine_schema, r.routine_name, r.specific_schema, r.specific_name, "
+                    + "r.spanner_type, r.routine_definition, r.security_type, r.spanner_determinism"
+                    + " FROM information_schema.routines AS r"
+                    + " WHERE r.routine_schema NOT IN"
+                    + " ('INFORMATION_SCHEMA', 'SPANNER_SYS')"
+                    + " AND r.routine_type = 'FUNCTION'"
+                    + " AND r.routine_body = 'SQL'");
body: Are these columns and tables of information schema rolled out and available for all PG databases in prod? If not we cannot merge this PR until it is rolled out. This applies to every query in this file. Please confirm.
I don't believe so, it's been about a year now, but to be safe I'll add it back.
Added a javadoc comment for the function
Added an example at the top of the function

body: Did you commit and push the code after adding the comment? I don't see the comment

I double-checked DdlTest.java and confirmed we have coverage for 0, 1, and multiple parameters across both dialects. I did find that the VOLATILE determinism type for PostgreSQL was missing, so I've added a new test case (pgUdfVolatile) to cover it
Looks like an unrelated change. Please revert this file.
functionSecutityType is column 7 and spannerDeterminism is column 8 . Isn't it? Am I missing something?
This block can execute for both GSQL and PG isn't it? We should just log `Cannot export user-defined functions.`

Do we need any other test cases here? The `parse` function in UdfParameter class looks very complicated and we should add thorough UT cases here. Maybe a couple of negative test cases should be added?
